research_tasks.task_title: Replot the optical setup schematic for polarization-resolved PL and HBT measurements
research_tasks.task_description: Use the provided Excel workbook to recreate the optical setup schematic associated with the polarization-resolved photoluminescence and Hanbury Brown–Twiss measurement configuration. Inspect all sheets in the workbook, identify any diagrammatic or annotated layout data, and reproduce the figure as a clean schematic-style visualization. If the workbook contains multiple panels or subcomponents, combine them into a single figure matching the original supplementary schematic layout as closely as possible. Preserve labels, arrows, optical elements, and detector paths if present. If the data are not directly drawable as a schematic, generate a faithful annotated reconstruction from the available structured content and clearly label all components.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and list all sheets, identifying any that contain schematic coordinates, labels, or diagram elements rather than numeric measurements.
Step_1: Extract all relevant objects, labels, and positional information needed to reconstruct the optical path and detection layout.
Step_2: Recreate the schematic as a publication-style figure with consistent typography, arrows, and panel formatting.
Step_3: Verify that the final plot includes all major optical components and measurement branches represented in the workbook.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A clean schematic figure showing the optical setup for polarization-resolved PL and HBT measurements, suitable for direct comparison with the supplementary schematic.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot control PL spectra and polarization analysis for unstrained and unindented samples
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the control photoluminescence spectra and polarization-analysis plots for reference samples. Identify the sheets containing PL spectra from unstrained WSe2, unindented WSe2/NiPS3, and any linear-polarization-resolved measurements of control and NiPS3-related peaks. Replot the spectra with the correct axes, legends, and channel separation. If the workbook includes derived polarization metrics, include them in the same multi-panel figure or as a companion panel. The goal is to reproduce the control comparison figure showing the absence or suppression of circular polarization in the reference cases and the linear polarization behavior of selected peaks.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect all sheets and identify control spectra and polarization-analysis tables.
Step_1: Standardize axis labels and channel names, then plot the spectra for each control condition.
Step_2: If linear polarization data are present, compute or plot the polarization dependence and summarize peak anisotropy.
Step_3: Assemble the panels into a publication-style comparison figure with clear legends and consistent color coding.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A multi-panel control figure comparing PL spectra and polarization behavior in reference samples, showing the relevant control trends and polarization metrics.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot control nanoindentation and excitation-polarization invariance spectra
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the supplementary figure showing a control nanoindentation on WSe2/hBN/PMMA and the excitation-polarization invariance of chiral PL in WSe2/NiPS3. Identify the sheets corresponding to the control indentation spectra and the excitation-polarization-dependent spectra. Replot the spectra in a consistent format, preserving any excitation labels, polarization channels, and peak assignments. If the workbook contains multiple traces for different excitation conditions, overlay them with a shared energy axis and annotate the comparison clearly.
execution_steps: Step_0: Locate the sheets containing control indentation spectra and excitation-polarization-dependent PL traces.
Step_1: Extract the energy axis and all polarization/excitation-condition channels.
Step_2: Plot the spectra for each condition on shared axes, using consistent normalization if required by the dataset.
Step_3: Add annotations or legends that make the invariance or comparison across excitation conditions visually clear.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A supplementary figure showing the control indentation comparison and excitation-polarization-dependent spectra, formatted as a replot-ready multi-panel plot.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the spectral image and spatial analysis of localized peaks at a nanoindentation
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the spectral-image figure for a nanoindentation, including the spatial map of emission and the analysis of multiple localized peaks. Identify the sheet(s) containing the spectral image matrix, spatial coordinates, and any fitted peak-position or amplitude summaries. Reconstruct the 2D spectral map and, if present, extract the peak positions or line cuts used for the spatial analysis. The final output should match the supplementary figure that combines a spectral image with quantitative analysis of several localized emitters or peaks.
execution_steps: Step_0: Identify the spectral image matrix and any accompanying coordinate or fit-summary sheets.
Step_1: Reconstruct the 2D spectral map using the correct axes and intensity values.
Step_2: Extract any peak-fit parameters or spatially resolved peak metrics and plot them alongside the spectral image.
Step_3: Format the result as a publication-style multi-panel figure with clear axis labels and peak annotations.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A spectral-image figure showing the nanoindentation emission map and the associated spatial/peak analysis of multiple localized peaks.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the optical image of the WSe2/NiPS3 heterostructure sample layout
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the optical image figure showing the WSe2/NiPS3 heterostructure and the sample layout. Search the workbook for image-like matrices, coordinate grids, or annotated region labels that correspond to the sample overview. Reconstruct the image as a publication-quality panel, preserving overlap regions, boundaries, and any marked indentation locations if present. If the workbook includes multiple related image panels, combine them into a single figure with consistent scaling and labeling.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the workbook for image matrices or coordinate tables that represent the sample overview.
Step_1: Determine the spatial extent and any region labels or boundaries from the available data.
Step_2: Render the optical image using an appropriate colormap or grayscale representation, adding annotations for key regions.
Step_3: Confirm that the final panel visually communicates the heterostructure layout and overlap geometry.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A clean optical overview image of the WSe2/NiPS3 heterostructure sample layout, suitable for direct figure reproduction.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the wide-field PL image showing quenched overlap emission and bright nanoindentation spots
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the wide-field photoluminescence image at low temperature that contrasts quenched emission in the overlap region with bright emission at nanoindentations. Identify the image matrix and any coordinate information needed to render the field of view. Replot the image with the correct intensity scaling and annotate the overlap region and indentation sites if those labels are present. The final figure should clearly show the spatial contrast between suppressed and restored PL emission.
execution_steps: Step_0: Locate the wide-field PL image data and any spatial coordinate tables.
Step_1: Reconstruct the 2D PL intensity map with appropriate contrast scaling.
Step_2: Add region annotations or outlines for the overlap area and indentation points if available.
Step_3: Format the panel as a publication-style image with a colorbar and clear spatial axes.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A wide-field PL image showing quenched emission in the overlap region and bright localized emission at nanoindentations.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the AFM topography and indentation cross-section
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the AFM topography panel and the representative cross-section through a nanoindentation. Identify the height map, any line-scan or profile data, and any coordinate grids needed to render the topography. Reconstruct the topography image and overlay the cross-section line or extract the corresponding height profile. If the workbook contains both schematic and measured data, combine them into a single figure that shows the indentation morphology and its depth profile.
execution_steps: Step_0: Find the height-image data and any line-profile or cross-section tables in the workbook.
Step_1: Reconstruct the 2D AFM topography map using the correct spatial axes.
Step_2: Extract the indentation cross-section profile and plot it as a line scan with depth scale.
Step_3: Combine the map and profile into a multi-panel figure with clear annotations and units if available.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A figure containing the AFM topography of a nanoindentation and a representative cross-sectional height profile.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot representative polarization-resolved PL spectra from individual nanoindentations
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the representative polarization-resolved photoluminescence spectra from individual nanoindentations. Identify the sheets containing energy-resolved spectra for the relevant polarization channels, including circular polarization components and any derived degree of circular polarization. Replot the spectra with clear channel separation, consistent normalization, and annotations of the localized emission peaks. If multiple nanoindentation spectra are present, arrange them as a multi-panel figure corresponding to the representative examples.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the workbook for spectral sheets with energy axes and polarization-resolved channels.
Step_1: Standardize the channel names and plot each spectrum against energy.
Step_2: If DCP is included, plot it or annotate it alongside the spectra for each indentation.
Step_3: Assemble the representative spectra into a publication-style figure with panel labels and legends.

ground_truth_outcome.description: Representative polarization-resolved PL spectra from individual nanoindentations, showing localized peaks and strong circular polarization.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot circular-polarization-resolved PL spectra under σ+ and σ− excitation
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the circular-polarization-resolved PL spectra measured under σ+ and σ− excitation for an indented region. Identify the sheets corresponding to each excitation condition and the emitted polarization channels. Replot the spectra on common axes so that the excitation-dependent response can be directly compared. Preserve any peak labels or intensity normalization used in the dataset, and ensure the final figure clearly distinguishes σ+ excitation from σ− excitation.
execution_steps: Step_0: Locate the sheets for σ+ and σ− excitation conditions and identify the emitted channels.
Step_1: Extract the energy axis and plot the spectra for each excitation condition on shared axes.
Step_2: Normalize or scale traces consistently if the workbook indicates that the data are replot-ready normalized spectra.
Step_3: Add legends and annotations to emphasize the comparison between excitation polarizations.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A comparison figure of PL spectra under σ+ and σ− excitation for the same indented region, suitable for direct figure reproduction.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the PL intensity time trace for a selected quantum emitter
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the PL intensity versus time trace for a selected quantum emitter. Identify the time-series sheet, extract the time axis and count/intensity values, and plot the trace with the same temporal resolution and normalization as provided. If the workbook includes multiple traces or repeated measurements, select the one corresponding to the representative emitter and preserve any smoothing or binning already present in the data. The final plot should match the time-trace panel used to characterize emitter stability or blinking behavior.
execution_steps: Step_0: Find the time-series table containing time and count/intensity values.
Step_1: Clean the time axis and handle any missing or placeholder entries.
Step_2: Plot intensity versus time using the dataset’s native sampling and normalization.
Step_3: If multiple traces exist, identify the representative one and format the figure accordingly.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A time trace of PL intensity for a selected quantum emitter, showing its temporal fluctuations or stability over the measurement window.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the time-resolved PL decay curve and fit
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the time-resolved photoluminescence decay curve. Identify the decay-time sheet, extract the time-delay or time axis and the normalized counts, and reproduce the decay trace. If the workbook includes fit parameters or a fitted curve, overlay the fit on the measured data and preserve the reported lifetime extraction. The final figure should present the decay on a suitable linear or semilog scale consistent with the dataset and include the fitted lifetime behavior.
execution_steps: Step_0: Locate the decay-curve sheet with time-delay or time axis and normalized counts.
Step_1: Plot the measured decay data and inspect whether a fit curve or fit parameters are provided.
Step_2: If a fit is present, overlay it and extract the characteristic lifetime from the provided values or by refitting.
Step_3: Format the plot to match a publication-style decay panel with clear axes and legend.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A time-resolved PL decay plot with the measured decay and fitted lifetime curve, reproducing the decay panel.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot the second-order photon correlation g(2)(τ) trace
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the second-order photon correlation trace used to demonstrate single-photon emission. Identify the correlation sheet, extract the delay axis and coincidence/count values, and plot g(2)(τ) in the standard symmetric format around zero delay. If the workbook includes normalization or background correction, preserve it in the replot. If fit parameters or a central antibunching dip are provided, annotate the plot accordingly. The final output should be a clean correlation curve suitable for direct comparison with the supplementary single-photon-emission figure.
execution_steps: Step_0: Find the sheet containing time-delay or correlation data for g(2)(τ).
Step_1: Plot the correlation counts versus delay, ensuring the trace is centered at zero delay if applicable.
Step_2: Apply any provided normalization or background correction from the workbook.
Step_3: Add fit or annotation elements that highlight the antibunching dip and overall correlation behavior.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A second-order correlation curve showing antibunching behavior, formatted as a publication-quality g(2)(τ) plot.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot field-dependent polarization-resolved PL spectra and DCP trends
research_tasks.task_description: Use the workbook to recreate the magnetic-field-dependent polarization-resolved PL spectra and the associated field trends in energy splitting and degree of circular polarization. Identify the sheets containing spectra at multiple positive and negative magnetic fields, as well as any summary tables for splitting and DCP. Replot the spectra as a field series, then generate the quantitative trend plot showing splitting and DCP versus magnetic field. If the workbook also includes temperature- or field-history-dependent spectra, include those as companion panels only if they are part of the same figure file. The final result should reproduce the magnetic-field dependence figure with both spectral evolution and extracted metrics.
execution_steps: Step_0: Inspect the workbook for field-indexed spectral sheets and any summary tables of splitting or DCP.
Step_1: Replot the spectra for each magnetic field value using a consistent energy axis and color scheme.
Step_2: Extract or compute peak splitting and DCP as functions of magnetic field from the provided tables.
Step_3: Combine the spectral series and trend plots into a multi-panel publication figure with clear field labels.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A multi-panel magnetic-field figure showing polarization-resolved PL spectra across field values and the corresponding energy-splitting/DCP trends.
